item.title: A weather balloon recorded footage of an airplane flying underneath it.
item.dc:creator: bohund

item.title: Tsunami Cloud outside Anna, Illinois
item.description: A tsunami cloud, or a cloud tsunami is really another name for a shelf cloud or a roll cloud, both ominous-looking clouds that can develop ahead of approaching thunderstorms. A shelf cloud is a low-level wedge-shaped cloud associated with the outflow or gust front from the approaching thunderstorm.
item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: Press Secretary Leavitt had a stunning response to French MEP Raphaël Glucksmann’s statement that France should reclaim the Statue of Liberty because the US no longer upholds its original values.
item.description: Dear Americans, Since the White House press secretary is attacking me today, I wanted to tell you this: 1. Our two people are intimately linked by History, the blood we shed and the passion for freedom we share, a passion symbolized by this Statue that was offered to the United States by France to honor your glorious Revolution. 2. As the press secretary for this shameful Administration said: without your nation, France would have "spoken German." In my case, it goes further: I would simply not be here if hundreds of thousands of young Americans had not landed on our beaches in Normandy. 3. Our gratitude to these heroes and their sacrifices is therefore eternal. 4. But the America of these heroes fought against tyrants, it did not flatter them. It was the enemy of fascism, not the friend of Putin. It helped the resistance and didn't attack Zelensky. 5. It celebrated science and didn't fire researchers for using banned words. It welcomed the persecuted and didn't target them. It was far, so far from what your current President does, says, and embodies. 6. This America, faithful to the wonderful words inscribed on the Statue of Liberty, your America, is worth so much more than the betrayal of Ukraine and Europe, xenophobia, or obscurantism. 7. We all in Europe love this nation to which we know we owe so much. It will rise again. You will rise again. We are counting on you. 8. And it is precisely because I am petrified by Trump’s betrayal that I said yesterday in a rally that we could symbolically take back the Statue of Liberty if your government despised everything it symbolizes in your eyes, ours, and those of the world. It was a wake-up call. 9. No one, of course, will come and steal the Statue of Liberty. The statue is yours. But what it embodies belongs to everyone. And if the free world no longer interests your government, then we will take up the torch, here in Europe.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.dc:creator: GOWskooma

item.title: Fun anecdote from the OG Oblivion development
item.description: MOST VIRAL edit: I hope everyone is enjoying the remastered edition. Oblivion is a game that I have a strange relationship with. It came in a time when I was attending uni and didn’t play many games, so I largely missed it (also the story was less compelling to me than other similar titles in the past, I like my existential dread to be more terrestrial). Attempts since then to play it have ended in failure because it’s so dated and I was definitely a big Skyrim guy, so I always just played that. I’ve always loved the world of Nirn, tho, and so now I’m stoked to have a chance to go back and give this game a proper playthrough!
item.dc:creator: TheOlDickTwist

item.title: What Happened Today #84
item.description: ? They still think they’re in charge. We see their panic. Courts crushed Trump’s crackdown today, blocking his attacks on voting, immigration, public schools, and civil rights. The Pentagon is leaking. DOJ lawyers are faceplanting. Trump is posting desperate pleas to Russia from his phone. Meanwhile they’re selling "Trump 2028" hats and picking fights with Canada. The mask is slipping. They don’t have a plan. They’re grasping at straws. And just like a toddler who thinks they're fooling their parents, it's painfully obvious. Don’t trust the algorithm. Get this in your inbox every night, free and private, so you never miss what’s really going on. Add your email at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/citizenship-proof-voting 2. https://go.govbrief.today/sanctuary-city-funding 3. https://go.govbrief.today/school-funding-dei 4. https://go.govbrief.today/scotus-transgender-military 5. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-unsecured-line 6. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-cos-quits 7. https://go.govbrief.today/vladimir-stop 8. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-russia-concession 9. https://go.govbrief.today/second-migrant-return 10. https://go.govbrief.today/act-blue-order 11. https://go.govbrief.today/probationary-employees-order 12. https://go.govbrief.today/no-warrant-khalil 13. https://go.govbrief.today/venezuelans-12-hours 14. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-fiore-pardon 15. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-carney-state 16. https://go.govbrief.today/burck-fired 17. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-2028-hats 18. https://go.govbrief.today/congestion-internal-memo

item.title: Images of Note: ifunny watermark
item.description:
(this is never a coincidence)
Thomas has had enough of Dr Beeching's shit
Oh, it's even worse: * Under the ruling: transgender people can be banned from any space or event, including mixed sex events. You can ban transgender people from Disabled toilets, single stalls and even just events like model rail shows. Doesn't need to relate to gender. * Transgender men can be forced into the womens. That's right, If you were born female, you can be forced into the women's UNLESS you look too masculine in which case you can be banned from any bathroom. This law LITERALLY MANDATES that certain men be forced into the women's. * All of this can be done with vibes. Are you a cis woman but butch? You can be banned from the women's. Look ugly? Banned from the women's. Don't perform femininity right? You guessed it, banned from the women's. Same if you're an androgynous male. Does it protect cisgender women from assaults? No, because predatory men don't care about the law and won't bother with what clothing they wear to assault women, nor will they upend their lives and transition just to assault people and get arrested for it. Fuck the UK.
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging http://bsky.abbii.gay: For Bluesky http://onlyfans.abbii.gay: for Tips!
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: Tolkien lore – armours of the Fellowship
item.description:
This is an account on the protective gear worn by the Fellowship members from that they set out on their quest until the hobbits came home.
Tolkien wrote on the topic of outfits "I visualize with great clarity and detail scenery and 'natural' objects, but not artefacts" (Letter 211); why clothes and armours are seldom brought up. Boromir came to Rivendell during the night to the Council of Elrond. He is described with a travelling cloak, garments and boots, but no kind of mail. Departing with the Fellowship his only protective gear specified was a shield, though he had one more item later mentioned (art by Joshua Cairos).
Before the Fellowship left Bilbo gave his marvellously light mithril mail shirt to Frodo as a secret gift (The Fellowship of the Ring; II; III onwards, art by Anke Eißmann): "It was close-woven of many rings, as supple almost as linen, cold as ice, and harder than steel. It shone like moonlit silver, and was studded with white gems."
In Rivendell Aragorn wore an Elf-mail once but he did not bring it when the Fellowship departed (art by Jenny Dolfen): "The Company took little gear of war, for their hope was in secrecy not in battle. Aragorn had Andúril but no other weapon, and he went forth clad only in rusty green and brown, as a Ranger of the wilderness."
There was one exception in the company, apart from Frodo with his hidden mail: "Gimli the dwarf alone wore openly a short shirt of steel-rings, for dwarves make light of burdens; and in his belt was a broad-bladed axe." Gimli had no helmet however, as will be seen in Rohan (art by Angus McBride).
ln the 2001 film adaptation Boromir has a mail shirt and is only pierced by three massive arrows. In the book he was "pierced with many black-feathered arrows" (The Two Towers; II; I), perhaps after his shield was cloven. When laid to rest his "helm they set beside him", here first mentioned (art by Anke Eißmann).
Legolas and Aragorn got armours in Edoras, capital of Rohan, in preparation for what became the battle at Helm's Deep. King Théoden said: "`And to you my other guests I will offer such things as may be found in my armoury. Swords you do not need, but there are helms and coats of mail of cunning work, gifts to my fathers out of Gondor. Choose from these ere we go, and may they serve you well!' Now men came bearing raiment of war from the king's hoard and they arrayed Aragorn and Legolas in shining mail. Helms too they chose, and round shields..." – TTT; III; VI (art by YoritomoDaishogun)
"Gandalf took no armour; and Gimli needed no coat of rings, even if one had been found to match his stature, for there was no hauberk in the hoards of Edoras of better make than his short corslet forged beneath the Mountain in the North. But he chose a cap of iron and leather that fitted well upon his round head; and a small shield he also took." –ibid The shield had the emblem of the House of Eorl for it had been Théoden's as a boy, and Gimli was proud to bear the device he told the King (art by John Howe).
Merry Brandybuck was on the request of Aragorn outfitted for battle by Éowyn (RotK; V; III with my bracket, art by Lisa Ing): "Now she led Merry to a booth among the lodges of the king’s guard and there an armourer brought out to her a small helm, and a round shield, and other gear. ‘No mail have we to fit you,’ said Éowyn, ‘nor any time for the forging of such a hauberk; but here is also a stout jerkin of leather, a belt, and a knife. A sword you have.’ Merry bowed, and the lady showed him the [green] shield, which was like the shield that had been given to Gimli, and it bore on it the device of the white horse."
In Gondor Pippin Took swore to serve the Steward and became a citadel guard. So he was "arrayed in strange garments, all of black and silver. He had a small hauberk, its rings forged of steel, maybe, yet black as jet; and a high-crowned helm with small raven-wings on either side, set with a silver star in the centre of the circlet. Above the mail was a short surcoat of black, but broidered on the breast in silver with the token of the Tree" (The Return of the King; V; IV). If he was outfitted as other Gondorian soldiers for the battle at the Black Gate, as would be reasonable, he was outfitted with a shield there (art by PineappleLightt).
Some wonder how Shelob stung Frodo when he had his mithril mail? The simple answer is that it lacked a hood, so Shelob "with one swift stroke had stung him in the neck" (TTT; IV; X). At Cirith Ungol Frodo lost his gear. Sam found a helmet and a dark cloak to disguise himself and for Frodo Orc clothes and "a coat of stout ring-mail, short for a full-sized orc, too long for Frodo and heavy." He added "a black cap with iron rim, and iron hoops covered with leather upon which the evil Eye was painted in red above the beaklike nose-guard" (RotK; VI; I). Both picked up shields (art by Borja Pindado).
After the fall of Sauron Sam awoke to find himself cared for by his friends, and the Host of the West waited to praise him and Frodo (RotK; VI; IV, art by Miriam Ellis): "Then Gandalf came and in his arms, to the wonder of Frodo, he bore the sword and the elven-cloak and the mithril-coat that had been taken from him in Mordor. For Sam he brought a coat of gilded mail, and his elven-cloak all healed of the soils and hurts that it had suffered;"
About a month later was Aragorn crowned king (RotK; VI; V, art by Inger Edelfeldt): "He was clad in black mail girt with silver, and he wore a long mantle of pure white clasped at the throat with a great jewel of green that shone from afar; but his head was bare save for a star upon his forehead bound by a slender fillet of silver."
Finally, it is plausible that also Merry at some time was provided a metal coat, either in Gondor or in Rohan, for when the four hobbits rode home towards the Shire people looked at them with amazement (RotK; VI; VII, art by Torbjörn Källström): "They themselves had become so used to warfare and to riding in well-arrayed companies that they had quite forgotten that the bright mail peeping from under their cloaks, and the helms of Gondor and the Mark, and the fair devices on their shields, would seem outlandish in their own country."
In case you enjoyed this and would like to read further Tolkien lore posts about characters, themes, objects and events, here are my indexes: Character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi
